rulings.Case Title: SAINT IGNATIUS NEIGHBORHOOD ASSOCIATION, VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL (CEQA Case)
rulings.Rulings: On CEQA Motion Calendar for Monday, March 11, 2024 in Department 301, Line 1. 2. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's (City's) Motion to Strike or Tax Costs is GRANTED, in part and DENIED, in part: " The Court STRIKES $3,444 in costs for the 57.4 hours that Plaintiff spent preparing the administrative record prior to stipulating with the City to prepare the record. " The Court TAXES $5,700 in costs for 95 hours that Plaintiff spent preparing the record as unnecessary and unreasonable. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on March 11, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional. Remote appearances may be via Zoom at a link to be provided by the Department 301 clerk. If a party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter, who must be licensed under Bus. & Prof. Code, sec. 8016. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. =(301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: SAINT IGNATIUS NEIGHBORHOOD ASSOCIATION, VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL (CEQA Case)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: On CEQA Motion Calendar for Monday, March 11, 2024 in Department 301, Line 1. 1. Plaintiff's Motion for Attorney Fees is GRANTED, in part and DENIED, in part. Plaintiff is a "successful party" within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure section 1021.5 because it has succeeded in achieving additional environmental review under the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA). On that basis, the Court awards attorney fees to Plaintiff based on the following calculations: o The Court REDUCES the attorney rate to $550 per hour as the prevailing rate for attorneys handling CEQA exemptions. o The Court EXCLUDES the 42.4 attorney hours and the 20.2 paralegal hours spent on administrative proceedings as not required prior to bringing an action based on CEQA exemptions which have no public hearing requirements. o The Court EXCLUDES the 11 attorney hours and 2.2 paralegal hours spent on publishing the appellate court decision because Plaintiff was not a "successful party." o The Court GRANTS the attorney fees for the 32 hours Plaintiff spent preparing this Motion and its Reply. o The Court DENIES the request for a multiplier and applies a negative multiplier of .80 because awarding attorney fees in this matter will ultimately fall upon the taxpayers. o Based on the foregoing, the Court grants Plaintiff's Motion for Attorney Fees in the amount $232,114.51. o Liability in this matter will be joint and several among Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Saint Ignatius College Preparatory School. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on March 11, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional. Remote appearances may be via Zoom at a link to be provided by the Department 301 clerk. If a party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter, who must be licensed under Bus. & Prof. Code, sec. 8016. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. =(301/RCE)
